mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and real personal income confirms broad-based economic expansion, increasing corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production indicates contracting manufacturing output that immediately pressures equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative following INDPRO declines during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction in contraction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 3.5% forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with a p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust job creation that supports consumer demand and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor strength signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity that immediately boost equity market sentiment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST increases over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer prices and falling industrial production creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises while INDPRO declines month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in over 65% of stagflationary divergence instances, or the joint condition fails to predict return direction with statistical significance.
